prompt: An authentic Japanese Edo-period ukiyo-e woodblock print of a solitary traveler descending a steep, terraced mountain. The mountain features stylized topographic lines resembling a contour map. The traveler is carefully stepping down the steepest, most direct path toward a deep, peaceful valley at the bottom. Rendered with Katsushika Hokusai compositional diagonals, flat areas of unmodulated color, visible woodgrain texture, stark figure-ground contrast, and a historically accurate muted palette of indigo, ochre, and pale vermilion. Absolutely no 3D shading, no modern lighting, no text, no calligraphy, no floating elements. Purely traditional 18th-century woodblock techniques.
tradition_approach: Targeted flat unmodulated colors, visible woodgrain texture, strong compositional diagonals, and a muted indigo/ochre palette to mimic traditional relief printing, strictly avoiding modern 3D shading.
